narrative:
Leandro Paredes is a key defensive midfielder for Argentina. His overall rating of 7.11/10 places him in the top 5% of defensive midfields at this tournament. This rating is a blend, with 53% from his club form over the last two seasons and 47% from this World Cup.

Paredes shows a strong defensive presence, evidenced by 1.607 tackles and 2.812 clearances per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His attacking output is modest, with 0.018 expected goals and 0.148 expected assists per 90 minutes in the tournament. He also averages 0.804 shots per 90 minutes at this World Cup.

Paredes serves as a crucial anchor in Argentina's midfield, protecting the backline. With 224 World Cup minutes played, his influence is felt in defensive transitions. Scouts should monitor his continued defensive work rate and how he dictates play in deeper areas for the remainder of the tournament.
